quark-cheese-fritters-in-the-air-fryer-p.name: Quark Cheese Fritters In The Air Fryer
quark-cheese-fritters-in-the-air-fryer-p.original_name: Buñuelos de queso quark en la freidora de aire
instructions: In a bowl, we mix well quark cheese, two eggs, vanilla sugar and salt. In a separate bowl we mixed the chemical leaven with the flour and added them little by little to the mzecla we had with the quark cheese and mixed them all well. The dough's got to get something sticky.

We put billing paper into the air fryer basket. With our hands slightly grown, we form small mass balls about 30 g and place them in the basket of the air fryer with sufficient space with each other as the balls will grow in size as they are baked. To form balls, it's important to have his hands slightly pitted as his dough's quite sticky. We can also help ourselves with two spoons to shape them or with an ice cream ball spoon. In any event we have also to be perfectly round about them, but what's important is that they don't have to be very big as they'll grow up and bake and stick with each other.

We program the air fryer at 180 and 3 and bake for 12 minutes, till they are golden. When it takes about 8 minutes, we open the basket of the air fryer and turn them to the quark frills to achieve a golden uniform.

After 12 minutes we get quarkbällchen out of the air fryer and let them cool briefly. We slightly heat up the butter till it melts. With a Silicone brush we paint quark balls with melted butter and then bounce them into sugar. We can also sprinkle some tidal sugar rather than bounce them into sugar. The quark frills are especially delicious hot freshly extracted from the air fryer with a drink as a cocoa or hot chocolate.

quinoa-and-ground-turkey-meatballs.name: Quinoa And Ground Turkey Meatballs
instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and spray a little nonstick spray on it.
Mix water and chia seeds together in a bowl to soak; set aside for 15 minutes. Stir after a few minutes to make sure all of the chia seeds are soaking up the water.
Finely chop onions, carrots, celery, spinach, and garlic in a food processor.

Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté vegetable mixture over medium heat until softened, about 8 minutes. Set it aside and let it cool a bit before mixing with other ingredients, about 10 minutes.

Mix turkey, quinoa, eggs, soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce, pepper, and salt together in a large bowl. Add the chia seed and water mixture (it should be sort of firm, almost like gelatin). Mix in the cooled vegetable mixture.
Shape turkey mixture into 48 meatballs and place onto the prepared pan. Brush with ketchup.
Bake in the preheated oven until no longer pink in the centers, 20 to 25 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 74°C (74 degrees C).

quinoa-and-roasted-vegetable-salad.name: Quinoa And Roasted Vegetable Salad

ingredients.item: Leftover quinoa with walnuts and shallots
Tender salad greens such as spinach

instructions: Toss roasted vegetables, quinoa, and greens with mustard dressing in a large bowl. Season with salt and pepper.

quinoa-pilaf-with-veggies-and-chickpeas.name: Quinoa Pilaf With Veggies And Chickpeas

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Combine chicken stock and quinoa in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer until quinoa is tender, about 15 minutes. Remove from heat.

Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering. Add asparagus, bell peppers, and onion; cook and stir until softened, about 6 minutes. Stir in chickpeas. Pour in 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar and cook until reduced, about 4 minutes. Add lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Stir in cooked quinoa. Drizzle 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar over pilaf before serving.
